message:
test(e2e_semantic_search): pin minimum fsvi file size instead of `> 0`

semantic_index_builds_vector_file (tests/e2e_semantic_search.rs:178)
previously asserted `metadata.len() > 0` on the produced .fsvi
vector index file. A regression that creates the file but writes
zero vector rows (e.g., the embedder fails mid-batch and the file
is opened, headers written, then closed before any embeddings
land) would produce a small but non-empty file and still pass.

Upgraded to `file_bytes >= 1024` with a named diagnostic. A real
fsvi index for even a tiny seeded corpus carries vector headers,
metadata, and embedding rows — a few KiB minimum. 1 KiB is the
conservative floor that catches the "created but empty" regression
without being flaky on micro-corpora.

Validation: all 57 tests in tests/e2e_semantic_search.rs pass
locally via CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cass_pane3.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
